summary: Decompiled and substantially improved matching for MSL math logf by rewriting the function around explicit IEEE-754 bit manipulation and moving math lookup tables out of src/MSL/math.c into a new src/MSL/math_data.c compilation unit. The PR accepted a known .rodata regression because separating the tables prevented mwcc from hoisting shared array base addresses, raising logf from about 59.82% to 99.80% matched with all 91 target instructions identical except for a stack-frame-size mismatch.

key_files.role: Reworked logf implementation and replaced in-file lookup table definitions with extern declarations for tables and bit-pattern coefficients.
New compilation unit holding __ln_F, __one_over_F, __sincos_on_quadrant, __sincos_poly, and logf coefficient bit constants.
Moved the MSL math .rodata range from MSL/math.c to new MSL/math_data.c split entry.

what_changed: Added a new MSL/math_data.c file containing math lookup tables previously defined in math.c: __ln_F, __one_over_F, __sincos_on_quadrant, and __sincos_poly.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so the .rodata range 0x803B8F50-0x803B93A0 belongs to MSL/math_data.c instead of MSL/math.c.
Changed math.c to declare the lookup tables as extern const float arrays instead of defining them locally.

Added extern u32 bit-pattern constants __logf_C0_bits and __logf_C1_bits for logf Taylor-series coefficients, with comments indicating they are intended to produce lwz global loads before reinterpretation as floats.

Rewrote logf(float x) to use explicit raw exponent, mantissa, and sign-bit handling, including exp_bits, mant_bits, raw_fm/raw_fM construction, table-index m, and special cases for NaN, +/-inf, subnormal, and zero.
Introduced a FloatU32 union typedef, though the shown diff primarily continues to use pointer casts and the F32_BIT_CAST macro.
Changed the polynomial expression structure for the normal-number path into named temporaries such as C0, C1, new_var, poly, and h2_poly to influence mwcc codegen.
Removed extensive old inline assembly-address comments from math.c and replaced them with higher-level comments explaining why tables live in math_data.c.
smart_moves: Separated lookup-table data into its own compilation unit to alter compiler codegen: the PR notes that tables in math.c caused mwcc to hoist array base addresses, while external tables forced separate address loads closer to the target.

Accepted a localized data-section regression as an explicit trade-off because it dramatically improved the function match: tables in math.c gave .rodata 100% and logf 81%, while tables in math_data.c gave .rodata 0% and logf 99.8%.
Stored logf coefficients as u32 bit patterns rather than float literals so mwcc would emit integer global loads followed by float reinterpretation, matching the target load style more closely.
Used explicit mantissa and exponent masks and intermediate raw float bit patterns to keep the source aligned with the target algorithm and instruction ordering.

Documented the remaining mismatch precisely: all 91 instructions were reported identical, with only stack frame size differing, attributed to mwcc allocating stack for float temporaries that should live in registers.
decomp_lessons: For MSL math functions, placing lookup tables in the same C file as the function can change optimization behavior by allowing base-address hoisting; moving tables to a separate compilation unit can improve function matching even if it hurts data matching.
A decomp PR may reasonably trade .rodata matching for text matching when the trade-off is documented and the remaining mismatch is well understood.
When float constants need target-like load behavior, define them as u32 bit patterns and reinterpret them locally instead of relying on float literals or local float arrays.
Small expression-structure changes and named temporaries can matter for mwcc register allocation, stack allocation, and instruction ordering in math code.
Track section ownership in splits.txt whenever data is moved between compilation units; otherwise match reports will conflate code progress with data-regression side effects.
For bit-level float routines, macros for exponent, mantissa, sign bit, and raw-float casting provide a reusable framework for matching original IEEE-754 manipulation code.
naming_conventions: Lookup-table symbols use double-underscore MSL-style names: __ln_F, __one_over_F, __sincos_on_quadrant, __sincos_poly.
Bit-pattern constants for float coefficients were named with a _bits suffix: __logf_C0_bits and __logf_C1_bits.
Raw integer representations of floats use raw_ prefixes, as in raw_x, raw_fm, and raw_fM.
Intermediate exponent and mantissa variables use descriptive names such as exp_bits and mant_bits.
Float bit-helper macros use uppercase F32_* names: F32_BIT_CAST, F32_UNBIASED_EXPONENT, F32_HIGH_MANTISSA_BITS, and F32_LOW_MANTISSA_BITS.
The separated data file was named math_data.c, matching its role as a companion compilation unit for MSL math table data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move const tables out of the function's compilation unit to prevent mwcc from hoisting shared array base addresses.
Use extern table references so each table access may generate separate address loads more like the original code.
Represent float coefficients as u32 globals to force lwz-style loads before casting to float.
Order assignments deliberately: load __logf_C0_bits, read raw_x, then load __logf_C1_bits, then derive mantissa/exponent fields.
Break polynomial math into temporaries rather than one large expression to guide instruction ordering and register allocation.
Use raw bit masks EXP_MASK, MANT_MASK, SIGN_BIT, EXP_ZERO, and high/low mantissa extraction to match target branch and table-index logic.
Known remaining issue was stack frame size: reported 64 bytes generated versus 40 bytes target, likely from mwcc stack allocation for float temporaries.

review_feedback.feedback: The PR had a known .rodata regression: matched data dropped by 1104 bytes and main/MSL/math .rodata went from 100.00% to 0.00%.
The same report showed logf improving from 59.82% to 99.80%, with all 91 instructions identical and only stack frame size differing.
Future work suggested exploring ways to prevent address hoisting while keeping tables in math.c, possibly via volatile, different array access patterns, or linker tricks.
No inline review comments were present in the supplied slice; specific reviewer intent beyond the merge and report excerpt is not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that solve the logf stack frame size mismatch of 64 vs 40 bytes.
Search for later changes that move MSL math lookup tables back into math.c without address hoisting.
Look for other decomp PRs using u32 bit-pattern globals to force lwz loads for float constants.
Search for uses of volatile, alternative array indexing, or linker tricks to prevent mwcc table base hoisting.
Check whether src/MSL/math_data.c remains split separately or is later consolidated.
